rules.description: Opening choices prefer mobility, compact disc shape, low frontier exposure, and avoidance of early unstable edge commitments. Early moves should not chase large flip counts unless they also improve stability.
Middlegame search values legal move differential, stable discs, access to corners, X/C-square risk, and opponent mobility restriction.

Corner ownership and connected edge extensions create discs that are hard or impossible to flip. Prefer stable extensions and avoid giving the opponent access to the corner through X/C-square weaknesses.
Endgame play prioritizes exact solver results when possible, parity, region control, forced sequences, and final disc count rather than early material greed.
